title: narcissus hedraeanthus subsp. luteolentus
intro: Narcissus hedraeanthus subsp. luteolentus is a perennial plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, known for its bulbous roots and clusters of small, yellow flowers. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties due to its alkaloid content, which may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Narcissus hedraeanthus subsp. luteolentus include potential uses as an antiseptic, a diuretic, and an expectorant, making it a possible remedy for respiratory issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant may be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
